name: Manjaro
description: Manjaro sits in a unique sweet spot: it uses Arch Linux as its base — giving access to the AUR and rolling updates — but adds a user-friendly graphical installer, hardware detection, and a slight delay before pushing packages to let the community catch issues first. Multiple official flavours with KDE Plasma, GNOME, and Xfce make it easy to start with a polished desktop. A great stepping stone toward Arch.
